utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains five items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five items are present. Each includes relevant details and costs: health_coverage specifies plan, cost, and coverage; property_coverage specifies coverage and limits; travel_coverage includes features (though no cost is listed, total_budget provides overall cost info); additional_services lists items with costs implied; total_budget includes monthly/annual costs. All are sufficiently detailed and aligned with the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant selected the 'LifeFirst Executive Balance Plan (plan_option28)' for health coverage. In the ground-truth ratings, only 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' exists, which likely corresponds to a base version of the same provider. For physical health, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' has a rating of 8, and for mental health, the same plan also has a rating of 8. Therefore, the health coverage maps to rating 8. The assistant also included property coverage with luxury items and business equipment—this aligns most closely with 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection.home_content, which has a rating of 8. For travel coverage, the plan includes global travel protection with medical evacuation and business travel protection—closest to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' in mobility_coverage.travel_insurance, which has a rating of 7. The additional services (concierge, dedicated agent, executive liability) collectively resemble premium-level services but do not correspond to a distinct rated item, so they are not rated separately. Thus, the mapping includes the three main coverages: health, property, and travel, using their corresponding 'LifeFirst' plan ratings.
